metadata.title: BRIGHT LITTLE NEWS ITEMS WHICH SHOW JUST WHAT SPOKANE DOING

pages.raw_text:
Special Commissioner Walter G.
dry farming congress. which is to
meet in Spokane October to 6, is
making a tour of the state and
speaking at all towns of interest.
He will speak at the following
pieces: Reardan. Davenport, Viii.
bur, Hartline and Coulee City this
week His itinerary for next week
is as follows: Waterville, May 3d.;
Wenatchee, May "a: Ephrata, May
25; Wilson Creek, May 26; Odessa,
May q, and Harrington, May 2S.
A meeting Of the Gonzaga college
alumni will be held at that institu-
ton on May 80.


A noon luncheon was given yes.
, terday at the chamber of commerce
to boost for the dry farming con-
gress and the national apple show.
The following visitors were pres
emit: T. E. Hamilton, Portland; H.
E. King. Lewiston. Idaho; w. K.
Lewis, Dayton; William Snyder,
Lind; L. c. Crow, Palouse; w. G..
Brandenburg, Denver; r. p. Cross,
Chicago; T. D. Shannon, Salt Lake;
F. M. Bigelow, New York, and Sem
ator R.C McCroskey, Garfield.


To fiji the vacancy Of L. Mclean,
leading business man of this city
and a member of the board of trus-
tees of the chamber of commerce,
F. d. Dewart was unanimously
elected and has been informed of
his nomination and election.


Prot. w. s. Bittner, who was op-
Grated on for appendicitis at the
Deaconess hospital last week, re-
turned to his classroom at the Spo-
kane college yesterday.


Three teachers Of the South Gen.
trail high school have served notifi-
cation on the Spokane school board
that they will not apply for their
positions next year. They are Miss
Caroline E. Henderson, instructor
in domestic science; C. c. Dill of
the English department, and c. Olin
Rice Of the music department.


The closing exercises Of the Spo-
Kane college will take place on
June II at the academy auditorium.
Wednesday, June s, has been an
nounced as class day. Former Con-
eressman Burton l.. French Of
Idaho will deliver the commence
ment address, while DR.. P. NJ. Gla-
soc will give the baccalaureate SER
mon Sunday, June s, at the college.


A new $4,000 telephone substa-
ton will be erected at Union Park
by the Pacific States Telephone &
Telegraph Co. to relieve the con-
gested situation of the city. The
contract has been let to ]. w. For
rest, who will start work at once.
Twenty thousand dollars worth of
modern equipment will be installed.
Mrs. Nellie Schy, whose husband,
George A. Schy, is suing for a d.
vorce, testified that her husband
was in the habit of engaging spies
to flirt with her. thereby aiding him
to file divorce proceedings.


Hugh L. Mekelvey, teanster, 10
on trial for his life before Judge
d. H. Carey for the murder of Oscar
Johnson on January last at the
Umatilla house.


r. D. speck, former sheriff Of
SDokane county. states that hummer
crops will be harvested throughout
the Palouse country.


Representative Secretary Robert
p. Cross OF the United States Land
and Irrigation exposition, to be held
at Chicago In November. under the
auspices of the Chicago Tribune, IL
